notes: 44% of sampled jobs have $0 totalExTax — invoiced externally (insurer-direct) or non-billable
waterCategory and waterClass not returned by Ascora API — must be inferred from jobDescription NLP
lineItems not available via API — contact Ascora support for correct endpoint
Highest-value job: $371,143 commercial water damage, Wooloowin QLD

water.description: Burst pipes, flexi-hose failures, appliance overflows, roof leaks with internal water intrusion
water.seasonalPeak: Summer (Dec–Feb) storm season and spring plumbing failures

mould_remediation.description: Microbial growth resulting from chronic moisture, delayed water damage response, or HVAC condensation

biohazard.description: Sewage backflows, unattended deaths, trauma scenes, sharps cleanup — Category 3 water classification
biohazard.ppeLevel: Level B minimum (full-face respirator, impermeable coveralls)
biohazard.regulatoryNotes: Safe Work Australia guidance; local council disposal pathway required

fire_smoke.description: Kitchen fires, electrical fires, structural fires with smoke migration. High-value due to combined remediation + contents + structural works
fire_smoke.typicalSmokeType: dry smoke (overheated cooking) and wet smoke (slow-burning organic material)

contents.description: Pack-out, cleaning, storage and pack-back of household and commercial contents following water, fire, or mould events
contents.iicrcStandard: S520 / S770 / S500:2025 (dependent on loss type)

storm.description: Wind and hail damage to roofs, fences, gutters; water intrusion from breached building envelope

inspection_only.description: Standalone moisture surveys, mould assessments, or pre-reinstatement clearance inspections — no remediation

other.description: General cleaning, odour treatment, trauma cleaning, miscellaneous restoration works not fitting primary categories

micro.description: Spot treatments, single-appliance overflow, minor mould spot (< 0.3 m²), inspection-only visits
exampleJobs: Category 1 dishwasher overflow, vinyl plank flooring only, Class 1 drying
Single bathroom mould wipe-down, Condition 2, <0.3 m²
Post-plumber moisture check — no active drying required

small.description: Single-room water damage drying, minor mould remediation (< 2 m²), small contents jobs
exampleJobs: Cat 1 flexi-hose burst under kitchen sink, 12 m² kitchen, Class 2 drying, 5 days
Bathroom exhaust fan failure causing mould, Condition 2, 1.2 m² plasterboard
Roof leak into single bedroom ceiling, Class 1, 3-day dry

medium.description: Multi-room water damage, significant mould remediation, storm damage with water intrusion, small biohazard events
exampleJobs: Cat 2 washing machine overflow, kitchen + laundry + hallway 45 m², Class 3, 10-day dry
Condition 3 mould to bathroom + ensuite wall cavity, 6 m² plasterboard removal
Hailstorm roof breach, 3-room water intrusion, 42 m² ceiling + insulation replacement

large.description: Whole-house flooding, major fire/smoke events, complex mould jobs, multi-unit residential
exampleJobs: Cat 3 sewer backup, whole-house ground floor 140 m², full biohazard protocol + structural drying
Kitchen fire with smoke migration throughout dwelling, S770 scope + contents pack-out
Multi-unit Cat 2 overflow affecting 3 townhouses, 180 m² total affected area

complex.description: Commercial losses, heritage-listed properties, catastrophic events, multi-trade coordination with structural engineers
exampleJobs: Commercial water damage Wooloowin warehouse, $371K, multi-floor, multi-trade
Large mould remediation Forest Lake, $117K, entire building envelope affected
Heritage Queenslander fire + smoke, full structural assessment required, NCC 2022 exemption process

commonScopeItems: Initial moisture survey with Tramex CME5 or equivalent (§10.1)
Emergency water extraction — carpet, hard floor, subfloor (§8.2)
Content protection — wet goods relocated or elevated (§8.3)
Anti-microbial application to all Cat 2/3 wet porous surfaces (§5.3/§5.4)
Flood cut at 450 mm — open wall cavities for airflow (Class 2/3)
Saturated insulation removal (glasswool/polyester) — mandatory if wet
LGR dehumidifier placement per IICRC S500:2025 equipment ratios
Air mover positioning: 45-degree angle to wet wall panels (§7)
Psychrometric baseline — temperature, RH, GPP, dew point (§10.2)
Daily monitoring log — moisture readings per material per zone (§11.4)
Drying goal certificate when all readings ≤ IICRC S500:2025 §11.4 EMC targets
keyRisks: Secondary mould growth if drying not commenced within 24 hours (Category 1→2 upgrade risk)
Category upgrade: Category 1 water sitting >72 hours becomes Category 2 per S500:2025
Hidden moisture in wall cavities causing delayed mould discovery
Asbestos-containing materials in pre-1990 construction (cornice, textured ceilings, vinyl tiles)
Hardwood flooring cupping or crowning if subfloor saturated — Class 4 protocol required
Electrical hazard: water ingress near switchboards — Licensed electrician assessment required
Multi-storey water migration — upstairs event affecting ground floor via stud cavities
iicrcReferences: S500:2025 §4 — Loss pathway and source identification
AS/NZS 3012:2019 — 80% electrical load for restoration equipment

equipmentRatios.lgrDehumidifier: 1 unit per 40 m² affected floor area
equipmentRatios.airMover: 1 unit per 15 m² OR 1 per wet wall panel (use higher count)

australianContextNotes: QLD climate (high ambient RH) increases drying time — adjust psychrometric targets accordingly
Hardwood sub-floors (particularly in Queenslander homes) require extended Class 4 protocols
Common flexi-hose failure: braided stainless over rubber, aged 7–10 years — insurer flags this
Split-system A/C condensate drain failures common in QLD summer — Cat 1 source

commonScopeItems: Visual assessment — contamination mapping by room, Condition 1/2/3 (§4.2)
Moisture source investigation — mandatory before any remediation (§5.1)
Contamination boundary — visible growth + mandatory 500 mm clearance (§6.3)
Condition 3: full critical barrier setup, negative pressure ≥12.5 Pa (§7)
HEPA air scrubber placement inside containment during active remediation
Wet wipe non-porous surfaces with EPA-registered antimicrobial then HEPA vacuum (§8)
Porous material removal (plasterboard, insulation, carpet) for Condition 3 — no cleaning alternative
Double-bag waste in 200 µm poly, label biological waste (§8.4)
Post-remediation HEPA vacuum of all surfaces within containment
Post-remediation clearance — visual inspection; independent hygienist air sample for Condition 3 (§9)
keyRisks: Unresolved moisture source invalidates remediation — scope must address source first
Condition 2 misclassified as Condition 1 — insurer dispute risk
Cross-contamination from inadequate containment in multi-room events
HVAC duct contamination spreading spores — system must be isolated or decontaminated
Asbestos co-located with mould growth in pre-1990 wall linings
Structural mould (timber framing) requiring borate treatment or replacement

commonScopeItems: Category 3 water classification — all porous materials in contact must be removed
Level B PPE deployment — full-face respirator P3, impermeable coveralls, double nitrile gloves
Occupant vacate order — health advisory issued in writing
All contaminated porous structural materials removed — no exceptions for Cat 3
EPA-registered broad-spectrum antimicrobial application to all surfaces
Air scrubber with HEPA filtration mandatory throughout
Waste disposal — double-bag 200 µm poly, council-approved biological waste pathway
Independent hygienist clearance testing required before reinstatement
Licensed waste contractor engagement for infectious waste
keyRisks: Under-scoping: Cat 3 water source means all wet porous materials must go — no exceptions
Sewage backup from council main — council liability dispute possible
Secondary contamination pathway via HVAC or drain lines
Occupant health obligations under WHS Act (Safe Work Australia)
iicrcReferences: Safe Work Australia — Managing Risks of Hazardous Chemicals / Biological Hazards

commonScopeItems: Fire origin and cause documentation — reference fire brigade report (§4.1)
Smoke zone mapping: char / smoke / odour by room (§4.2)
Dry sponge technique for dry smoke soot — prior to any wet cleaning (§8)
Enzyme-based cleaner for protein smoke (cooking residue) (§8)
HEPA vacuum all surfaces before and after wet cleaning (§8)
Thermal fogging for odour penetration into wall cavities and sub-floor (§10)
Ozone treatment — specify room, duration (minimum 4 hours), re-entry interval (§10)
Hydroxyl generator placement — note rooms and treatment days (§10)
HVAC isolation and decontamination — duct system smoke migration assessment (§4.2)
Contents inventory — restore vs replace per item, ACL replacement value methodology (§9)
Structural drying if suppression water present — S500:2025 Category 1 protocol applies
Air quality clearance test (Condition 1 restored) — independent hygienist (§11)
keyRisks: Smoke odour return after ozone/fogging — indicates incomplete coverage of cavities
Hidden char in wall framing (fire path not fully mapped)
Asbestos-containing materials in pre-1990 homes disturbed by fire/fire suppression
HVAC system spreading smoke/soot to non-fire-affected areas
Heritage property — NCC 2022 exemption process, heritage architect engagement required
Insurance dispute over contents — restore vs replace threshold documentation critical
iicrcReferences: S770 §8 — Cleaning methods by smoke type and material

commonScopeItems: Breach identification — roof, windows, gutters, doors (§ AS 4055:2021 if structural)
Emergency make-safe: tarpaulin over breach within 24 hours (ICA obligation)
Temporary protection: 200 µm poly tarpaulin, 4 m overlap, screwed timber battens
Roof space inspection: sarking, battens, insulation saturation level
Insulation removal if saturated — glasswool/polyester cannot be dried in situ
Psychrometric survey — ceiling void and affected rooms (S500:2025 §10)
Structural drying per S500:2025 if water has entered structure
Plasterboard ceiling replacement per affected area (m²) — no partial patch for storm-soaked board
Tile replacement — like-for-like supply (match existing profile and colour)
Reinstatement to NCC 2022 standard — licensed roofing and plumbing trades
keyRisks: Delay in make-safe causing further water intrusion — ICA make-safe timeline breach
Matching discontinued tile profile — heritage or aged properties
Concealed roof truss damage from hail or falling debris
Asbestos cement sheeting or fibrous plaster on pre-1990 properties
Insurer dispute on partial vs full ceiling replacement scope
iicrcReferences: S500:2025 §7 — Evaporation class for storm water intrusion
AS 4055:2021 — Wind loads for housing (structural assessment trigger)
NCC 2022 Vol 2 — Reinstatement standard for Class 1 buildings (residential)

pricingDrivers: water category (Cat 3 ~2× Cat 1 for equivalent area)
damage class (Class 3/4 extends drying time and equipment count)

jobStatusInsights.notes: Peak in Q1 aligns with QLD storm season (Nov–Mar). Mould jobs peak in QLD winter (Jun–Aug) due to reduced ventilation and cold-bridge condensation.
